sn7.16:1.1: On one occasion the Blessed One was staying near Sāvatthī in Jeta’s Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s monastery.
sn7.16:1.2: And on that occasion a brahman named Paccanikasāta (Enjoyer of Contradiction) was living in Sāvatthī.
sn7.16:1.3: Then the thought occurred to the brahman Paccanikasāta,
sn7.16:1.6: Now, at that time the Blessed One was doing walking meditation in the open air.
sn7.16:1.7: So the brahman Paccanikasāta went to the Blessed One and, following behind the Blessed One as he was doing walking meditation, said to him,
sn7.16:2.4: who’s defiled in mind, intent on confrontation.
sn7.16:4.1: When this was said, the brahman Paccanikasāta said to the Blessed One,
sn7.16:4.2: “Magnificent, Master Gotama! Magnificent! Just as if he were to place upright what was overturned, to reveal what was hidden, to show the way to one who was lost, or to carry a lamp into the dark so that those with eyes could see forms, in the same way has Master Gotama—through many lines of reasoning—made the Dhamma clear. I go to Master Gotama for refuge, to the Dhamma, & to the Saṅgha of monks.
sn7.16:4.3: May Master Gotama remember me as a lay follower who has gone for refuge from this day forward, for life.”
